rcomplex 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Comparative co-expression analysis with rcomplex

Find co-expressologs across all species pairs

Source: R/comparison.R
find_coexpressologs.Rd

Runs the full comparison pipeline for each species pair and combines the results into a single edge data frame ready for find_cliques.

find_coexpressologs(networks, ...)

# Default S3 method
find_coexpressologs(
  networks,
  orthologs,
  species_pairs = NULL,
  method = c("analytical", "permutation"),
  alternative = c("greater", "less"),
  alpha = 0.05,
  n_cores = 1L,
  use_torch = FALSE,
  min_exceedances = 50L,
  max_permutations = 10000L,
  ...
)

run_pairwise_comparisons(...)

Arguments

networks

Named list of compute_network outputs, keyed by species abbreviation.

...

Additional arguments passed to the default method.

orthologs

Data frame with columns Species1, Species2, hog (from parse_orthologs or extract_orthologs).

species_pairs

Optional list of length-2 character vectors specifying which pairs to compare. Defaults to all combn(names(networks), 2).

method

Testing method: "analytical" (default, fast) or "permutation" (rigorous).

alternative

"greater" (conservation, default) or "less" (divergence).

alpha

Significance threshold (default 0.05).

n_cores

Number of threads (default 1).

use_torch

Logical. Use GPU-accelerated fold-enrichment precomputation for permutation method (default FALSE).

min_exceedances

Besag-Clifford stopping parameter for permutation method (default 50).

max_permutations

Maximum permutations for permutation method (default 10000).

Value

Data frame with columns gene1, gene2, species1, species2, hog, q.value, effect_size, type. Ready for find_cliques or classify_cliques.

Methods

analytical

Fast path: pair-level Storey q-values via summarize_comparison. Appropriate when most HOGs are single-copy.

permutation

Rigorous path: gene-identity permutation via permutation_hog_test with Besag-Clifford adaptive stopping and Liang discrete q-values. Required for multi-copy HOGs where pair-level tests are correlated.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Fast analytical path
edges <- find_coexpressologs(networks, orthologs)

# Rigorous permutation path with GPU
edges <- find_coexpressologs(networks, orthologs,
  method = "permutation", use_torch = TRUE, n_cores = 4L)
} # }

Contents
